page.content: In Table 1, the treatment conditions reveal distinct variations in biochemical markers across different doses and treatments. The "Normal" group shows ALT at 36.05 ± 10.52 U/L, AST at 124.3 ± 16.14 U/L, and ALP at 193.0 ± 41.44 U/L.

lines.text: In Table 1, the treatment conditions reveal distinct variations in biochemical markers across different doses and treatments. The
"Normal" group shows ALT at 36.05 ± 10.52 U/L, AST at 124.3 ± 16.14 U/L, and ALP at 193.0 ± 41.44 U/L.

page.content: The "10% DMSO + PCM" group exhibits significantly elevated levels with ALT at 1714 ± 142.2#, AST at 2266 ± 340.4#, and ALP at 330.0 ± 42.35#. The "NAC + PCM" treatment at a dose of 50 mg/kg results in ALT at 884.2 ± 195.4*, AST at 1569 ± 106.4*, and ALP at 284.3 ± 5.536*. For the "MEMC + PCM" group, also at 50 mg/kg, ALT levels are recorded at 2734 ± 495.2*, AST at 1292 ± 468.0*, and ALP at 311.5 ± 25.64. The 250 mg/kg dose shows ALT at 1638 ± 174.4, AST at 2565 ± 170.5, and ALP at 359.0 ± 32.73. The highest dose of 500 mg/kg results in ALT at 244.9 ± 101.9*, AST at 526.1 ± 191.1*, and ALP at 221.7 ± 25.55*. In Table 2, the "Performances activities" scenario is classified as Poor in the Optimal column and Good in the Worst column; "Energy-saving public facility" and "Energy-saving family" both rank Low in Optimal and Moderate in Worst; "Problem based learning" is noted as Common in Optimal and Hot in Worst; finally, the "Output value" is reported at 54.2 in Optimal and 74.3 in Worst.
